name: Robotic Vision Summer School
note: The program incorporates both talks and practical sessions run by world-leading researchers in robotics and computer vision. This is an international summer school which offers a tremendous opportunity to broaden the educational experience and professional networks for attendees.

name: Robot handling of deformable objects Winter School
note: The purpose of the winter school is to address the underlying topics of robot handling of deformables and get hands-on experience in the specifics of perception, simulation, use of robots, and applications in the field. We have invited the leading European scientists and practitioners in this field to give lectures and we have prepared interactive sessions / mini projects with the use of collaborative robots and simulation packages. The school is specifically crafted for young and talented Ph.D. students and post-docs.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia
note: The goal of the school is to bring together researchers from complementary fields—such as computer vision, speech/audio processing, and human-robot interaction—who share a common interest in social robotics. The event aims to foster cross-disciplinary exchange and discussion, both through formal courses and informal interactions.

name: SeaClear2.0 Winter School
note: This winter school aims at sharing the latest advancements in the SeaClear2.0 project and other marine robotics initiatives. Join us to explore cutting-edge developments in autonomous underwater systems designed to protect our oceans

name: Introduction to Piezoelectric Mechatronics
note: The purpose of this winter school is to introduce to piezoelectric mechatronic systems, from understanding the piezoelectricity principles to advanced modeling of certain systems. The last part of the school will be devoted to introduction to their control. Various applications examples will be presented as well. The winter school encompasses lectures and tutorials. The last day will be devoted to social event.

name: Winter School on Foundation Models
Spring School on Machine Learning and Perception for Social Awareness
note: Organized by the SWEET project consortium in conjunction with the TRAIL Conference, this Spring School offers a unique opportunity to deepen your knowledge in social perception for service robotics, with a special emphasis on cutting-edge machine learning, sensing technologies, and algorithmic approaches that enable robots to understand and interact with humans in socially aware ways.

name: International School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence
note: AIces 2026 will be the first in a series of research training events aiming at updating participants on the most recent multidisciplinary discussions about the foundations, meaning, challenges and risks of AI.

name: Event-driven Vision School
note: Event-driven cameras adoption in real-world applications is steadily growing, thanks to their low-power, low latency, high temporal resolution, high dynamic range and highly compressive encoding. The EVS school is the first event focused on teaching computer vision with event cameras. Its aim is to offer students an in-depth knowledge of state-of-the-art methods to process event-driven camera data and teach them the required, practical, skills to develop their own applications

name: Nengo Summer School
note: The Centre for Theoretical Neuroscience at the University of Waterloo is excited to announce our 11th annual Nengo summer school on large-scale brain modelling and neuromorphic computing. As a recurring special feature of the school, participants will be given on-site access to neuromorphic hardware, including Stanford’s mixed digital/analog chip Braindrop, the SpiNNaker chip, and a variety of FPGAs.

name: A Residential Course: From AI Agents to Agentic AI
note: ACDL 2026  is a full-immersion 5-day course in Riva del Sole Resort & SPA - Tuscany on cutting-edge advances in AI, Deep Learning, LLMs, Data Sc., Generative AI, AI Agents & Agentic AI  with lectures delivered by world-renowned experts

name: Summer School Machine Learning Crash Course
note: Machine Learning is key to develop intelligent systems and analyze data in science and engineering. Machine Learning systems enable intelligent technologies such as Siri, Google self-driving car, or Chat-GPT to name a few. At the same time, Machine Learning methods help deciphering the information in our DNA and make sense of the flood of information gathered on the web, forming the basis of a new “Science of Data”. This course introduces the fundamental methods at the core of modern Machine Learning. It covers theoretical foundations as well as essential algorithms.

name: 14th International UBI Summer School
note: UBISS 2026 invites researchers, students, and industry and public sector professionals to gain theoretical insight and personal hands on experience of selected topics on robotics under the tutelage of distinguished experts. UBISS 2026 continuec the legacy of the previous 13 successful multidisciplinary UBI Summer Schools hosted by us in Oulu in 2010-2025.

name: Summer Workshop on Surgical, Assistive, and Interventional Engineering
note: Surgical, Assistive, and Interventional Engineering is transforming healthcare by merging groundbreaking technology with medical innovation. Our Summer Workshop offers a unique opportunity to explore this exciting field through hands-on learning and expert guidance.

name: AURORA Rising - Robotics Summer School
costNote: acc. and meals incl.; scolarships available

name: ETH Robotics Summer School
note: Robotics has become one of the biggest fields of education and research worldwide. The RobotX center at ETH Zurich is committed to provide continued education and training to the young generations of researchers and practitioners. This summer school offers lectures and hands-on tutorials to grasp the fundamental concepts that every roboticist around the world should possess.

name: RPL Summer School
note: The summer school hosts a mix of academic and industrial researchers from around the world and aims to provide a forum that promotes new collaborations. Our school focuses on a diverse range of topics within robotics, perception, and learning, encompassing areas such as Machine Learning, Computer Vision, Robotics, Human-Robot Interaction, Planning, and Decision Making.

name: ICAPS 2026 Summer School
iRoboCity2030
1st Summer School in Robotics and AI
note: The goal of the summer school is to introduce students, Ph.D. candidates, and scientists/engineers at a post-doctoral level to some of the most recent state-of-the-art topics at the cross section of Robotics and AI. The scope and the content of the sessions also aim to address needs of engineers in the industrial and service sectors with a strong interest in Robotics.

name: RoboStar Summer School
note: The RoboStar Summer School is a four-day, in-person event, hosted at the University of York, dedicated to Software Engineering of trustworthy robotic systems. It brings together leading international researchers and industry practitioners to present the full range of RoboStar technologies, from modelling and simulation to deployment and assurance.

name: Machine Learning Summer School on Reliability and Safety
note: The school is organised as an intensive academic programme built around invited lectures delivered by a carefully selected group of researchers from academia and industry, whose work has significantly contributed to the development of the field. Speakers are actively involved in current research and are invited based on their scientific contributions and expertise.

name: 3rd Summer School for Systems & Software Engineering in Robotics
note: Our target audience includes PhD students and early-career professionals interested in systems and software engineering, as well as cognitive robotics.

name: International Computer Vision Summer School
note: The twentieth edition of the International Computer Vision Summer School aims to provide both an objective and clear overview and an in-depth analysis of the state-of-the-art research in Computer Vision. The last decade has seen a revolution in the theory and application of computer vision and machine learning. The next decade will see machine perception in embodied systems which learn representations for and from action and interaction for spatial intelligence.

name: AI for Human–Robot Interaction summer school
note: The AI for Human–Robot Interaction summer school offers an intensive 5-day program of keynotes, lectures and practical sessions led by experienced AI and robotics experts. Participants will engage with top researchers and will be tutored to use AI models and methods on real robots, advancing in their knowledge to deploy human–robot interaction systems grounded in recent advances in artificial intelligence. They will have the opportunity to engage with industry leaders building and deploying AI methods in the real world.

name: EuroHaptics School
note: It will focus on hands-on techniques and skills that are needed for running haptic research projects in academia and industry. The school aims to provide knowledge encompassing the ideation at the start of any project, prototyping methods to build experiments and devices, evaluation paradigms, and analysis methods. Keynotes, courses, and workshops will focus on specific topics of the project progress chain in both academic and industrial contexts. The school will encourage the application of the knowledge through participation in teams in a hackathon, as well as networking during social events. A dedicated session will be held to present and discuss your research with the other participants. The program will feature a mixture of keynotes from recognized haptic researchers in academia and industry (as well as exchange time with them), tutorials, and networking events.

name: IEEE Summer School on Telerobotics and Cyborg Technologies
Screw-Theory Based Methods in Robotics
note: The International Summer School on Screw-Theory-Based Methods in Robotics (Summer Screws) has been organized since 2009 and has been held ten times around the world. In 2026, our screw-theory summer school travels to the far end of Europe, embarking on an adventure in the Emerald Isle for the first time. A selection of our 8 renowned regular lecturers will join a group of local guest speakers and up to 40 participants at University College Cork (UCC), Cork, Ireland. As always, the school will teach attendees to apply existing screw-theoretical methods and empower them to develop new ones in their own research. This summer school is suitable for M.Sc. students, PhD students, postdoctoral researchers, and early-career academic staff, as well as participants from industry who wish to enhance their skills in this field.

name: 13th International School on Deep Learning
note: DeepLearn 2026 will be a research training event with a global scope aiming at updating participants on the most recent advances in the critical and fast developing area of deep learning.

name: IRCN Neuro-inspired Computation Summer Course
note: The World Premier International Research Center for Neurointelligence (WPI-IRCN) at The University of Tokyo proudly presents the Neuro-inspired Computation Course, an intensive 4-day program exploring the convergence of neuroscience and artificial intelligence. The course is comprising lectures by distinguished international faculty, poster sessions, and interactive discussion sessions. Beyond academic content, the program offers exceptional networking opportunities with global experts and peers, fostering collaboration and innovation. Participants will have the chance to present their research, exchange ideas, and explore future directions in neuro-inspired computation.

name: IEEE RAS Summer School on Multi-robot systems
note: Building on the success of our previous Summer Schools held in 2019, 2020, 2022, 2023, 2024 and 2025, we are pleased to announce the IEEE RAS Summer School 2026, which will take place from July 29 to August 4, 2026. As in previous years, the event will be hosted at the Czech Technical University, situated in the heart of one of Europe’s most beautiful and historic cities. The Summer School is dedicated to showcasing the latest advancements in Multi-Robot Systems research and is aimed at students, academic researchers, and industry professionals. The program seeks to bridge the gap between theory and practice, supporting the real-world application of cooperating robotic systems while fostering collaboration and knowledge exchange among participants.

name: Summer School to Enable Walking Robots for Domestic Environments
note: The FZI Research Center for Information Technology, in cooperation with Karlsruhe Institute of Technology, will organize a summer school to enable walking robots for domestic environments using 3D mapping, semantics, ML, ROS 2, and behavior trees for unknown and dynamic environments.

name: Touch Sensing and Processing Summer School
note: Compared to vision or hearing, touch is a poorly understood sensing modality. Thanks to recent advances in hardware development, it will soon be possible to digitize touch at scale. This creates important opportunities for both basic research and applications, such as robotics. In this context, it is increasingly important the role of Touch Processing – the science of making sense of touch through the use of machine learning.

name: The 4th Summer School On Parallel Robotics
note: The aim of this summer school is to share knowledge on the design, modeling, control, and applications of parallel kinematic manipulators over the course of one week, with a strong focus on realistic prototypes and real-world industrial challenges. The program consists of plenary lectures and hands-on simulation laboratories. The summer school is intended for PhD students, postdoctoral researchers, and researchers who are already active in the field or who have an  interest in parallel kinematic manipulators.

name: International Summer School on Legged Robots, from Basics to Frontier Research
Computational and Geometric Mechanics of Discrete and Continuum Systems
note: This course will introduce attendees to fundamental concepts and mathematical formulations of geometric mechanics and provide a panoramic overview of current state of research and applications of geometric modeling. It covers theoretical and mathematical foundations, computational methods that allow for practical application to multibody system dynamics, as well as cutting-edge multidisciplinary research.
